model.revision: macOS 26.5.2 system-managed asset; asset revision is not exposed
model.license: Apple platform asset; not redistributed by Cuttledoc

runtime.boundary: Repository-owned Swift C ABI called from Rust

options.input: caller-owned f32 mono converted to framework-required s16 mono

energy.notes: The repeatable powermetrics procedure is defined, but this baseline did not run with root privileges.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: No model conversion; the owned shim converts public f32 PCM to the Int16 buffers required by SpeechAnalyzer.
engineering_cost.update_cost: The shim compiles only against the Swift-only macOS 26 Speech API and must be retested with each supported Xcode/macOS line.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The optimized Swift dylib was 346992 bytes and the Rust probe 603816 bytes; production still needs a stable host bundle identity and normal signing/notarization.

result.blocker: The system asset revision, quantization, and byte size are not exposed; clean-host cold load and relative energy remain unmeasured, and one English fixture cannot select a product backend.
observations: The final transcript differed from the reference only by pluralizing the last word.
Twenty-seven volatile whole-range replacements were followed by one final update with 19 word time ranges and confidence values.
No empty result or replacement-free retraction was observed, so no Revoke update was emitted.
A separate es-ES lifecycle probe moved asset status from supported to installed, owned and released its reservation, and cancelled cleanly.
The ad-hoc unbundled executable worked, but Speech logged that the absent bundle identifier produces an unstable client identifier.
